paper_title: Marine Sediment Core Analysis from Antarctic Peninsula

methods.description: Process of recovering a marine sediment core from the Antarctic Peninsula to study past climate conditions and ice sheet dynamics.

steps.description: A whole round core (EAP13-GC17, 236 cm below the sea floor) of marine sediments was recovered (65°48.16' S, 60°39.42' W; 526 m) in the LIS-B embayment of the Antarctic Peninsula during a marine geological expedition in 2013.
steps.searchable_summary: recover marine sediment core from specified coordinates

methods.description: Examination of the sediment core to identify lithological units and measure isotopic compositions for paleo-climate reconstruction.

steps.description: The sediment core (EAP13-GC17) was subdivided into four distinct lithological units from top (U1) to bottom (U4) via X-ray radiograph images and optical observation.
steps.searchable_summary: subdivide sediment core into lithological units using X-ray and optical methods

steps.description: The isotopic compositions of δ13C and δ15N were measured for each lithological unit.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotopic compositions of δ13C and δ15N for lithological units

steps.description: Meteoric 10Be concentration was measured and found to increase gradually from the bottom of the sample upwards, reaching maximum values in the uppermost part of the core.
steps.searchable_summary: measure meteoric 10Be concentration in sediment core
